hub_subtitle: Business English from the ground up -- your work, your team, your routine

lesson.menu_desc: Compare tools, systems, and teams with comparative adjectives -- faster than, more reliable than -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 19 -- Comparatives -- Comparative Adjectives
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Danilo Febba | Aula 19 -- Comparatives

listenings.text: Hi, I am Rachel. Let me compare two tools we use on the team. The first one is Python. Python is faster than Excel for big files, and it is more reliable. The second one is our old reporting tool. It is simpler, but it is slower and less accurate. For small tasks, the old tool is easier. For large tasks, Python is much better. The new dashboard is also more modern than the old one, and it is more flexible. Python is more powerful, but the old tool is cheaper. So we use both.

Hello, this is Danilo. Let me compare our two servers. The new server is better than the old server. It is faster, and it is more powerful. The old server is slower, but it is cheaper. Our new pipeline is more reliable than the old pipeline, and it is more accurate. The old pipeline is more complex, and it is harder to fix. The new one is simpler and more efficient. The cloud option is more expensive than the local option, but it is more flexible. For me, the new server is the better choice.
